signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: GBP/AUD at 1.8826 — G8 scorecard identifies a critical bilateral theme with a 1.416% spread. GBP outperformance (+0.571%) vs AUD weakness (-0.846%) occurs despite a -60bp carry disadvantage (BoE 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%). AUD COT LF net long +55,794 (74.8%) suggests a crowded long liquidation is driving the underperformance. The bear flattener regime (2s10s +54.6bp) supports the higher-yielding USD/GBP complex over high-beta AUD.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5858 — COT LF net short -19,437 (22.3% long) shows speculators heavily fading the RBNZ's hawkish bias. With the RBNZ meeting on 27 May and a market bias for a hike, the pair faces significant short-squeeze risk if the central bank delivers. USD/NZD carry at +150bp currently favors USD, but crowded spec shorts are vulnerable to a hawkish surprise.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Volatility Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1608 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.9% generates a +2.2% Vol Risk Premium, signaling elevated institutional hedging demand. 25d RR at -0.47 confirms a mild put skew as markets price downside protection. COT LF net short -8,151 aligns with the 175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.00%), suggesting the path of least resistance remains lower toward 1.1550.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.12 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) sustains the bid despite JPY COT LF net short -70,605. 160.00 remains the critical watch level for BoJ verbal warnings or direct intervention. Options market is paying for downside protection with a -1.32 moderate put skew (25d RR), indicating institutional fear of a sharp reversal despite the carry advantage.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3427 — IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.9% creates a significant +4.1% VRP, the highest in the G10 space. Despite GBP being the G8 weekly leader, the -0.95 moderate put skew in 25d RR suggests the options market is aggressively hedging against a reversal. Carry is neutral (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%), leaving the pair sensitive to US yield shifts.
